item.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
item.description: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

item.title: Waste-to-energy plant proposed for Vuda Point in Fiji sparks fierce backlash
item.description: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

item.title: Authors ditch University of Queensland Press after Indigenous kids book pulled for 'anti-Semitism' of illustrator
item.description: Writers including Evelyn Araluen and Randa Abdel-Fattah have left University of Queensland Press after the publisher axed publication of an Indigenous kids book over its illustrator's past "anti-Semitic" comments.

item.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
item.description: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

item.title: UK raises terrorism threat level after stabbings as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer says Jews are scared
item.description: Britain raises its national terrorism threat level to "severe" from "substantial", a day after an antisemitic stabbing attack in north London, as Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vows stronger action to protect Jewish people.

item.title: Banksy confirmed behind new London statue of man blinded by flag
item.description: Elusive street artist Banksy has confirmed he is behind a new work drawing crowds in central London: a statue of a suited man striding into the void while holding a flag that blocks his view.
